headline: Extreme heat peaks 106°F — tip canes now, zones Saturday.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 8 AM through Monday — Extreme Heat Warning is active until July 14 with 100–106°F daily highs.
Run both zones early Saturday morning — yesterday's irrigation is 2 days dry by Saturday's 103°F, crossing the re-water threshold for extreme heat.
Check e1–e4 transplant soil between irrigation cycles — 20-day tomatoes under 100–106°F may need spot watering before the next scheduled zone run.
Check potato bag moisture daily through Monday — bags lose moisture 2–3× faster than ground beds in 100–106°F extreme heat.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — do it before 8 AM while it's coolest; Saturday's 103°F and Sunday's 106°F close every subsequent outdoor morning window through the weekend.
